steps.title: Join two main clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form one complete clause about the Limon technique's emphasis and interplay of weight and weightlessness. The words after the blank form another complete clause about what dancers may explore.

steps.title: Reject weak boundaries
steps.content: Choice A leaves the two independent clauses fused with no punctuation. Choice B uses which, which cannot join the two main clauses this way. Choice C uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. The answer is D.
